meta.note: This catalog is extracted directly from the Word job descriptions and should be treated as the baseline role/JD source of truth.

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/source_docs/Job Descriptions/Finance Director.docx

identity.Location (Site/Region/Group): Enterprise / Multi-site (Europe HQ with regular travel to production sites and sales subsidiaries)
identity.Direct Reports: 3–6 Finance Leads (e.g., Country Finance Leads, Group Accounting Lead, FP&A Lead, Tax & Treasury Lead); total org span 11–50 FTE across countries/sites

job_descriptions.role_summary: Leads the Finance function with enterprise-wide responsibility for financial strategy, performance management, and control across all entities. Designs and builds scalable finance capabilities (processes, systems, governance) to support high growth, M&A integration, and continuous improvement, while safeguarding liquidity, compliance, and value creation.

key_responsibilities.title: Financial Strategy & Planning
key_responsibilities.description: Lead the development of financial strategies, long‑term plans, budgets, and forecasts to support business objectives and growth ambitions across the group.

key_responsibilities.title: Performance Management & Controlling
key_responsibilities.description: Lead financial performance management (P&L, balance sheet, cash, capex) across plants and commercial entities; analyze variances versus budget/forecast and drive corrective actions with business leaders.

key_responsibilities.title: Cash Flow, Funding & Treasury
key_responsibilities.description: Lead cash flow planning, working capital optimization, and treasury activities; ensure adequate liquidity, efficient use of capital, and compliance with financing covenants.

key_responsibilities.title: Financial Reporting & Consolidation
key_responsibilities.description: Lead timely and accurate monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting; oversee consolidation of financial statements for all legal entities in line with applicable accounting standards.

key_responsibilities.title: Compliance, Tax & Audit
key_responsibilities.description: Ensure compliance with legal, tax, and regulatory requirements as well as applicable accounting frameworks; lead interactions with external auditors, tax advisors, regulatory authorities, and financial institutions.

key_responsibilities.title: Cost & Process Optimization
key_responsibilities.description: Identify and lead cost optimization, margin improvement, and efficiency initiatives; drive automation and digitalization of end‑to‑end finance processes (P2P, O2C, R2R).

key_responsibilities.title: Decision Support & Investment Analysis
key_responsibilities.description: Provide financial analysis and recommendations to the Executive Team and senior leaders; evaluate investment opportunities, business cases, and M&A transactions, including ROI and risk/return trade‑offs.

key_responsibilities.title: Risk Management & Internal Controls
key_responsibilities.description: Lead identification, assessment, and management of financial risks; design and maintain internal control frameworks and contingency plans to protect company assets and ensure stability.

key_responsibilities.title: Team Leadership & Cross-functional Collaboration
key_responsibilities.description: Lead, develop, and coach Finance Leads and teams across countries; ensure effective collaboration between finance and other functions (Operations, Supply Chain, Sales, HR, IT) to enable informed decision‑making.

key_responsibilities.title: Investor & Stakeholder Communication (if applicable)
key_responsibilities.description: Lead preparation of financial materials for presentations to investors, shareholders, banks, and other external stakeholders, ensuring transparency and consistency of financial information.

key_responsibilities.title: Illustrative KPIs
key_responsibilities.description: revenue and margin performance vs. plan, working capital and cash conversion, forecast accuracy, cost savings and productivity gains, audit findings, on‑time closing, automation/digitalization milestones, return on investments.

organizational_scope.Extent of Impact: Drives enterprise‑wide financial decisions and performance management; materially influences profitability, cash generation, investment capacity, and financial risk profile across all entities.

reporting_relationships.Matrix/Functional Reporting: Close partnership with Chief Executive Officer and Chief Operating Officer for strategy, performance, and investments; collaboration with HR, IT, Supply Chain, Sales, and Plant Leadership on cross‑functional projects and M&A.
reporting_relationships.Direct Reports (Number, Job Titles): 3–6 Finance Leads (e.g., Group Accounting Lead, FP&A Lead, Tax & Treasury Lead, Country Finance Leads)
reporting_relationships.Dotted-line/Influence Relationships: Site Leads, Shared Services/SSC Leads (if applicable), external auditors, tax advisors, banks and financial institutions.

technical_skills: Financial Risk Management & Internal Controls — Advanced
Finance Systems & Digitalization (ERP, consolidation tools, BI) — Advanced
technical_and_business_knowledge.business_knowledge_required: Strong understanding of manufacturing and supply chain economics (plants, inventory, logistics), commercial models (pricing, trade terms, channel margins), and industry dynamics in FMCG/food; solid knowledge of multi‑entity and multi‑country structures, including implications for tax, transfer pricing, and reporting.

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/source_docs/Job Descriptions/HR Director.docx

identity.Location (Site/Region/Group): Group / Multi-site (Europe HQ with frequent travel to production sites and sales subsidiaries)
identity.Direct Reports: 3-5 Country HR Leads and HR Experts; total org span 11–50 FTE across countries/sites

job_descriptions.role_summary: Leads the Group HR function with enterprise-wide impact on policies, standards, and people outcomes across all production sites and offices. Sets the People strategy to enable high growth, M&A integration, and culture harmonization in a dynamic, fast-paced environment—building scalable processes from the ground up while safeguarding compliance and workforce engagement.

key_responsibilities.title: People Strategy & Governance
key_responsibilities.description: Define and execute the Group People strategy (workforce planning, org design, culture, DEI) aligned to company growth and M&A roadmap to drive capability, engagement, and productivity.

key_responsibilities.title: Talent Acquisition & Employer Brand
key_responsibilities.description: Lead TA across countries to meet ramp-up needs; reduce time-to-fill and improve quality-of-hire; steward Group employer brand and partnerships.

key_responsibilities.title: Total Rewards & Performance
key_responsibilities.description: Own Group C&B frameworks (base pay, incentives, benefits) and performance management; ensure external competitiveness, internal equity, and pay-for-performance discipline.

key_responsibilities.title: HR Operations & Systems
key_responsibilities.description: Standardize HR policies, employee lifecycle processes, and HRIS/data across sites; deliver reliable payroll interfaces and self-service; drive data integrity and People analytics.

key_responsibilities.title: Learning, Leadership & Culture
key_responsibilities.description: Build L&D architecture (onboarding, technical/plant training, leadership academies) to support safety, quality, and continuous improvement; accelerate manager capability.

key_responsibilities.title: M&A and Integration
key_responsibilities.description: Lead HR due diligence, integration planning, and post-close harmonization (policies, grades, benefits, culture) to protect continuity and realize synergies.

key_responsibilities.title: Employee Relations & Compliance
key_responsibilities.description: Ensure compliant ER practices across jurisdictions (works councils/unions where present), health & safety collaboration, and audit readiness; mitigate labor risk.

key_responsibilities.title: Stakeholder Leadership & Change
key_responsibilities.description: Advise Executives and Senior Leaders; lead change communications and organization-wide initiatives (e.g., org redesign, restructures, new site start-ups).

organizational_scope.Extent of Impact: Drives enterprise-wide people decisions across all entities; materially influences workforce-related operating costs and compliance/employee-relations risk.

reporting_relationships.Matrix/Functional Reporting: Dotted line to Chief Executive Officer (Group); close collaboration with COO for plant-related workforce priorities; partnership with Legal/Compliance for ER/regulatory.
reporting_relationships.Direct Reports (Number, Job Titles): 5–8 Country HR Leads and HR Experts
reporting_relationships.Dotted-line/Influence Relationships: Plant Leadership, Sales Subsidiary Leaders, Works Councils/Union Representatives, external advisors.

technical_and_business_knowledge.business_knowledge_required: End-to-end understanding of plant and commercial operations in FMCG/food production; familiarity with BRC/IFS-driven environments and their HR implications; strong financial acumen (workforce cost modeling, productivity, ROI on People initiatives); experience navigating multi-entity, acquisition-driven environments.

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/source_docs/Job Descriptions/HR Lead.docx

identity.Direct Reports: HR Specialists / HR Administrators (number to be defined)

job_descriptions.role_summary: The HR Manager ensures effective implementation of HR strategies and policies at the site level, supporting talent acquisition, employee engagement, and compliance. This role drives cultural integration, process development, and workforce planning to enable operational excellence and growth in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.

key_responsibilities.description: Lead recruitment and workforce planning for the site to ensure timely staffing aligned with business needs and growth targets.
Manage employee relations, resolve conflicts, and implement engagement initiatives to foster a collaborative and inclusive culture.
Build and standardize HR processes and systems locally, ensuring alignment with group policies and compliance with labor regulations.
Drive performance review cycles, coach managers on feedback and development planning to enhance productivity and retention.
Ensure adherence to legal requirements, health and safety standards, and company policies to minimize operational and regulatory risks.
Support integration of acquired businesses and lead local change initiatives to align with organizational values and strategic priorities.

organizational_scope.Extent of Impact: Direct impact on site workforce efficiency, compliance, and engagement.
organizational_scope.Decision Autonomy: High autonomy on local HR operations; escalates strategic or policy changes to HR Director.

reporting_relationships.Matrix/Functional Reporting: Group HR (for policy alignment)
reporting_relationships.Dotted-line Relationships: Site leadership team, Finance, external HR service providers

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/source_docs/Job Descriptions/IT Director.docx

identity.Location (Site/Region/Group): Multi-site (Europe HQ with frequent travel to production sites and sales subsidiaries)
identity.Direct Reports: 5–8 Country IT Leads and IT Experts; total org span 11–50 FTE across countries/sites

job_descriptions.role_summary: Leads the IT function with enterprise-wide impact on technology strategy, policies, and platforms across all production sites and offices. Builds a scalable, secure, and data-enabled technology foundation to support high growth, M&A integration, and continuous improvement—standardizing the core stack while enabling local agility.

key_responsibilities.title: Technology Strategy & Governance
key_responsibilities.description: Define the IT strategy and operating model (architecture principles, standards, service catalog, SLAs, RACI) aligned with business growth and M&A roadmap.

key_responsibilities.title: Enterprise Architecture & Roadmaps
key_responsibilities.description: Set target architecture for core platforms (ERP, MES/SCADA, WMS/TMS, CRM, HRIS, Data/ Analytics) and lead multi-year modernization and consolidation roadmaps.

key_responsibilities.title: Cybersecurity & Risk
key_responsibilities.description: Establish enterprise security policies and controls (e.g., ISO 27001/NIST-aligned), manage risk assessments, incident response, and audit readiness across IT/OT environments.

key_responsibilities.title: Applications & Data Enablement
key_responsibilities.description: Govern application portfolio, master data, and integration patterns; enable analytics, automation, and AI/ML through a governed data platform and KPI suite.

key_responsibilities.title: Infrastructure & Connectivity
key_responsibilities.description: Standardize networks, identity & access, end-user services, and cloud/on‑prem hybrid infrastructure; ensure resilience, performance, disaster recovery (DR) and business continuity (BCP), and cost efficiency.

key_responsibilities.title: M&A and Integration
key_responsibilities.description: Lead IT due diligence, Day‑1 readiness, and post-close integration (systems, data, cyber, infrastructure) to minimize business disruption and realize synergies.

key_responsibilities.title: Vendor & Portfolio Management
key_responsibilities.description: Own strategic vendor selection, contracts, and performance; prioritize and steer the IT project/program portfolio to deliver value on time and budget.

key_responsibilities.title: Change Leadership & Stakeholder Management
key_responsibilities.description: Advise executives and site leaders; drive change communications, adoption, and training for technology-enabled transformations.

key_responsibilities.title: Illustrative KPIs
key_responsibilities.description: platform uptime & incident MTTR, cyber posture/controls coverage, on-time/on-budget delivery, ERP/MES rollout milestones, integration cycle time, cost-to-serve per user/site, audit findings, data quality SLAs.

organizational_scope.Extent of Impact: Drives enterprise-wide technology decisions across all entities; materially influences digital risk, operational continuity, and technology cost to serve.

reporting_relationships.Matrix/Functional Reporting: Dotted line to Chief Executive Officer; close partnership with COO and Plant Managers for OT/MES and site digitalization; Legal/Compliance for data protection.
reporting_relationships.Direct Reports (Number, Job Titles): 5–8 Country IT Managers and IT Experts
reporting_relationships.Dotted-line/Influence Relationships: Site/Local IT Leads, Data & Analytics teams, Business Process Owners (Finance, Supply Chain, Sales, HR), External MSPs/Vendors, Security/Privacy Officers.

technical_skills: ERP/MES/WMS Platforms (e.g., SAP, Microsoft Dynamics, Infor; MES/SCADA) — Advanced
Data Platform & Integration (ETL/iPaaS, APIs, MDM) — Advanced
technical_and_business_knowledge.business_knowledge_required: End-to-end understanding of manufacturing and supply chain processes (plan/make/move/sell), commercial applications, and compliance contexts; financial acumen for business cases and TCO; experience in multi-entity, acquisition-driven environments.

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/source_docs/Job Descriptions/Procurement Director.docx

identity.Location: Group (covering all production sites and sales subsidiaries)
identity.Direct Reports: Strategic Buyers

job_descriptions.role_summary: Leads the Group’s strategic procurement function, defining sourcing strategies, supplier partnerships, and risk management frameworks to ensure long-term competitiveness and sustainability. This role focuses on building procurement capabilities, driving cost optimization, and supporting growth initiatives such as M&A and new product development, rather than day-to-day operational purchasing.

key_responsibilities.title: Develop Group Procurement Strategy
key_responsibilities.description: Design and implement long-term sourcing strategies aligned with business growth, sustainability, and cost-efficiency objectives.

key_responsibilities.title: Supplier Portfolio Management
key_responsibilities.description: Establish and maintain strategic partnerships with global and regional suppliers to secure innovation, quality, and supply continuity.

key_responsibilities.title: Risk & Compliance Governance
key_responsibilities.description: Define procurement policies and risk management frameworks to ensure compliance with regulatory and certification standards (e.g., BRC, IFS).

key_responsibilities.title: Cost Optimization & Value Creation
key_responsibilities.description: Drive initiatives for cost reduction, process efficiency, and value-added supplier collaboration across all categories.

key_responsibilities.title: Support Growth & M&A Integration
key_responsibilities.description: Lead procurement due diligence and integration planning for acquisitions and new business units.

key_responsibilities.title: Digital Transformation
key_responsibilities.description: Implement advanced procurement tools (ERP, e-sourcing platforms) to enhance transparency, analytics, and decision-making.

key_responsibilities.title: Cross-functional Leadership
key_responsibilities.description: Collaborate with Operations, Finance, Quality, and R&D to align procurement strategies with production excellence and innovation goals.

organizational_scope.Extent of Impact: Direct influence on Group-level cost structure, supplier risk, and strategic growth initiatives.
organizational_scope.Decision Autonomy: High autonomy in defining procurement strategy, selecting strategic suppliers, and negotiating major contracts; decisions aligned with Group policies and executive direction.

reporting_relationships.Dotted-line Relationships: Plant Directors, Quality Assurance, Logistics, R&D

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/generated_final/Generated Job Descriptions Final/Application Manager and Smart Factory.docx

identity.Direct Reports: IFS Application Specialist and selected application or support resources as assigned

job_descriptions.role_summary: Leads the application landscape that connects plant operations, core business processes, and smart factory initiatives. Owns the roadmap for manufacturing-related applications, coordinates cross-functional adoption, and ensures that industrial digitalization is practical, governed, and tied to business value.

key_responsibilities.title: Application Ownership
key_responsibilities.description: Own the lifecycle, roadmap, and support model for selected industrial and operational applications, including interfaces to ERP and plant systems.

key_responsibilities.title: Smart Factory Roadmap
key_responsibilities.description: Define and prioritize smart factory use cases that improve visibility, data capture, workflow reliability, and operational decision-making.

key_responsibilities.title: Integration Coordination
key_responsibilities.description: Coordinate application and data flows across ERP, MES, shopfloor tools, reporting layers, and plant processes in partnership with IT and operations stakeholders.

key_responsibilities.title: Business Partnering
key_responsibilities.description: Act as the main point of contact for plant and operations teams on application needs, enhancements, adoption issues, and digitalization opportunities.

key_responsibilities.title: Governance & Change Management
key_responsibilities.description: Ensure that new applications, enhancements, and automations follow clear ownership, documentation, training, and release practices.

key_responsibilities.title: Vendor & Delivery Coordination
key_responsibilities.description: Coordinate external implementation partners, vendors, and internal IT resources to deliver sustainable changes on time and with acceptable supportability.

key_responsibilities.title: Operational Support Oversight
key_responsibilities.description: Oversee issue triage, prioritization, and escalation for critical industrial application issues that affect production or planning continuity.

key_responsibilities.title: Illustrative KPIs
key_responsibilities.description: application availability, issue resolution responsiveness, enhancement throughput, user adoption, and business impact of smart factory initiatives.

organizational_scope.Extent of Impact: Directly influences application reliability, digital workflow quality, and the success of industrial digitalization across plant-related use cases.
organizational_scope.Decision Autonomy: Makes application and roadmap decisions within approved architecture, budget, and governance boundaries; escalates major investment, security, or enterprise architecture impacts.

reporting_relationships.Matrix/Functional Reporting: Close partnership with Chief Operating Officer, Plant Director, Supply Chain, and Engineering leaders on industrial use cases and digital priorities.
reporting_relationships.Direct Reports (Number, Job Titles): IFS Application Specialist and selected application support or project resources as assigned
reporting_relationships.Dotted-line/Influence Relationships: Business Intelligence, Master Data, Production Planning, Quality, external integrators, and application vendors.

technical_skills: Smart Factory Use Case Design - Advanced
Business Process Translation into Digital Solutions - Advanced
technical_and_business_knowledge.business_knowledge_required: Strong understanding of how plant execution, planning, and business process workflows depend on applications, data quality, and practical user adoption.

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/generated_final/Generated Job Descriptions Final/Business Intelligence Developer.docx

identity.Direct Reports: No direct reports

job_descriptions.role_summary: Designs and delivers reporting, dashboards, and data models that help business and operational teams make better decisions. Translates business questions into usable analytics outputs while balancing data quality, usability, and maintainability across multiple stakeholder groups.

key_responsibilities.title: Dashboard & Report Delivery
key_responsibilities.description: Build and maintain reports, dashboards, and self-service analytics outputs for executive, functional, and operational users.

key_responsibilities.title: Data Modeling
key_responsibilities.description: Design and refine data models that support consistent KPI logic, reusable reporting structures, and practical business interpretation.

key_responsibilities.title: Requirements Translation
key_responsibilities.description: Work with business stakeholders to convert reporting needs into data, logic, and visualization requirements that can be delivered and sustained.

key_responsibilities.title: Data Quality Support
key_responsibilities.description: Identify data inconsistencies, logic gaps, and reporting issues; partner with source-system owners and data stewards to improve trust in analytics outputs.

key_responsibilities.title: Automation & Efficiency
key_responsibilities.description: Streamline recurring reporting flows, reduce manual effort, and improve the timeliness and reliability of key business reporting.

key_responsibilities.title: Documentation & Knowledge Sharing
key_responsibilities.description: Document logic, assumptions, and dependencies in a way that supports continuity, governance, and easier maintenance.

key_responsibilities.title: Cross-functional Analytics Support
key_responsibilities.description: Support reporting needs across Finance, Supply Chain, Commercial, HR, or Operations depending on business priorities.

key_responsibilities.title: Illustrative KPIs
key_responsibilities.description: dashboard adoption, report accuracy, delivery lead time, automation impact, stakeholder satisfaction, and KPI consistency.

organizational_scope.Extent of Impact: Improves decision quality across functions by delivering trusted, usable analytics assets and helping the organization work with clearer KPI logic.
organizational_scope.Decision Autonomy: Makes day-to-day design and technical choices within approved architecture and governance practices; escalates broader platform, source-data, or prioritization conflicts where needed.

reporting_relationships.Matrix/Functional Reporting: Works closely with Finance, Supply Chain, Commercial, Operations, and Master Data stakeholders depending on active reporting priorities.
reporting_relationships.Direct Reports (Number, Job Titles): No formal direct reports
reporting_relationships.Dotted-line/Influence Relationships: Data stewards, ERP/application owners, business controllers, planning teams, and key dashboard users.

technical_skills: Documentation & Reporting Governance - Intermediate to Advanced
technical_and_business_knowledge.business_knowledge_required: Good understanding of how reporting supports operational, financial, commercial, and planning decisions, including the importance of clear KPI ownership and data definitions.

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/generated_final/Generated Job Descriptions Final/Chief Operating Officer.docx

identity.Location (Site/Region/Group): Group / Multi-site (Europe HQ with frequent travel to production sites and sales subsidiaries)
identity.Direct Reports: Plant Director, Group Supply Chain Director, Head of Group Quality, senior operations leaders across sites

job_descriptions.role_summary: Leads the Group Operations agenda across manufacturing, supply chain execution, quality coordination, and operational transformation. Shapes the operating model needed for scale, resilience, and continuous improvement while aligning plant performance, service levels, and investment priorities with the company's growth ambitions.

key_responsibilities.title: Operations Strategy & Performance
key_responsibilities.description: Define and execute the Group operations strategy across plants and fulfillment activities; set the performance cadence, target metrics, and management routines that align local execution with enterprise goals.

key_responsibilities.title: Plant Governance
key_responsibilities.description: Oversee plant leadership across sites; ensure safe, stable, and efficient manufacturing operations, clear accountability, and disciplined escalation of production, labor, quality, and continuity issues.

key_responsibilities.title: Supply and Service Alignment
key_responsibilities.description: Coordinate with Supply Chain, Procurement, Commercial, and Finance to balance service, cost, inventory, and capacity trade-offs in support of business priorities.

key_responsibilities.title: Operational Excellence
key_responsibilities.description: Lead continuous improvement programs focused on throughput, waste reduction, labor productivity, planning discipline, and process standardization across the network.

key_responsibilities.title: Quality & Compliance Coordination
key_responsibilities.description: Ensure that quality, food safety, and operational compliance expectations are embedded into the operating model and consistently executed across sites.

key_responsibilities.title: Capex & Transformation Prioritization
key_responsibilities.description: Steer plant and operations investment priorities, including automation, expansion, asset replacement, and site improvement programs, to maximize business value and resilience.

key_responsibilities.title: M&A and Integration
key_responsibilities.description: Lead the operations workstream for acquisitions and integrations, including Day-1 readiness, manufacturing continuity, footprint alignment, and synergy execution.

key_responsibilities.title: Leadership & Change
key_responsibilities.description: Coach senior operations leaders, drive cross-site collaboration, and reinforce a culture of ownership, pace, and fact-based decision-making.

key_responsibilities.title: Illustrative KPIs
key_responsibilities.description: OTIF/service level, plant OEE, yield and waste, labor productivity, inventory health, quality incidents, safety metrics, capex delivery, and cost-to-serve.

organizational_scope.Extent of Impact: Drives enterprise-wide operating performance across manufacturing and supply execution; materially influences service, cost base, inventory, quality, and operational resilience.
organizational_scope.Decision Autonomy: Makes broad operational decisions within approved strategy and budget, including network priorities, performance expectations, operating routines, and transformation sequencing; escalates major structural, legal, or investment decisions for CEO/Board approval.

reporting_relationships.Matrix/Functional Reporting: Close partnership with Chief Financial Officer on performance and investment governance; partnership with HR, IT, Quality, Procurement, R&D, and Commercial on cross-functional priorities.
reporting_relationships.Direct Reports (Number, Job Titles): Plant Director, Group Supply Chain Director, Head of Group Quality, and selected senior operations leaders
reporting_relationships.Dotted-line/Influence Relationships: Engineering leaders, Planning leads, Logistics managers, local plant leadership teams, and external operational partners.

technical_skills: Capacity, Cost, and Service Trade-off Management - Advanced
technical_and_business_knowledge.business_knowledge_required: Strong understanding of plant operations, network planning, service models, procurement dependencies, and the cost/service implications of operational choices in a multi-entity environment.

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/generated_final/Generated Job Descriptions Final/Group Supply Chain Director.docx

identity.Department/Function: Supply Chain / Planning / Logistics / Customer Service
identity.Direct Reports: Production Planning Manager, logistics leaders, customer service leadership, and selected supply chain experts

job_descriptions.role_summary: Leads the Group supply chain agenda across planning, logistics, customer service, and supply coordination. Balances service, inventory, capacity, and execution constraints to create a more reliable, responsive, and scalable supply model across markets and sites.

key_responsibilities.title: Supply Chain Strategy
key_responsibilities.description: Define and execute the Group supply chain strategy across planning, logistics, and customer service in line with business growth and service ambitions.

key_responsibilities.title: Planning Governance
key_responsibilities.description: Establish the planning model, decision forums, and KPI routines needed to balance demand, production, inventory, and service trade-offs consistently.

key_responsibilities.title: Logistics & Fulfillment Oversight
key_responsibilities.description: Own the operating standards and performance expectations for logistics, transport coordination, and order fulfillment support.

key_responsibilities.title: Customer Service Integration
key_responsibilities.description: Ensure that customer service and supply execution work as one operating model with clear escalation pathways and realistic service commitments.

key_responsibilities.title: Inventory & Service Optimization
key_responsibilities.description: Improve inventory health, planning discipline, and supply responsiveness while protecting cash, service, and plant stability.

key_responsibilities.title: Cross-functional Coordination
key_responsibilities.description: Work closely with Commercial, Procurement, Operations, Finance, and IT on supply decisions, constraints, and operating improvements.

key_responsibilities.title: Transformation & Standardization
key_responsibilities.description: Lead programs that improve supply chain visibility, planning maturity, process consistency, and digital support for decision-making.

key_responsibilities.title: Illustrative KPIs
key_responsibilities.description: OTIF, inventory health, forecast bias/accuracy, planning adherence, logistics service, order issue rate, and supply chain cost performance.

organizational_scope.Extent of Impact: Directly influences service performance, inventory, planning quality, logistics efficiency, and operational responsiveness across the company.
organizational_scope.Decision Autonomy: Makes broad supply chain and planning decisions within approved budgets and enterprise priorities; escalates major structural or investment decisions where needed.

reporting_relationships.Matrix/Functional Reporting: Close partnership with Commercial, Procurement, Plant Leadership, Finance, and IT on service, inventory, and planning trade-offs.
reporting_relationships.Direct Reports (Number, Job Titles): Production Planning Manager, logistics and customer service leaders, and selected Group supply chain experts
reporting_relationships.Dotted-line/Influence Relationships: Country commercial teams, plant planners, warehouse teams, transport partners, and supply chain analysts.

technical_and_business_knowledge.business_knowledge_required: Strong understanding of how demand, planning, plant capacity, procurement constraints, customer commitments, and financial objectives interact in an integrated supply model.

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/generated_final/Generated Job Descriptions Final/Head of Engineering and Reliability.docx

identity.Direct Reports: Project and Process Engineers, maintenance planning specialists, selected technical experts

job_descriptions.role_summary: Leads technical reliability, engineering governance, and operational asset improvement for the plant. Builds the maintenance and engineering roadmap needed to improve uptime, technical stability, and delivery of process change while balancing capex, operational risk, and production realities.

key_responsibilities.title: Reliability Strategy
key_responsibilities.description: Define and execute the plant reliability strategy for critical assets, maintenance planning, preventive routines, and technical risk reduction.

key_responsibilities.title: Engineering Governance
key_responsibilities.description: Own engineering standards, technical documentation expectations, change control practices, and practical decision frameworks for modifications and upgrades.

key_responsibilities.title: Maintenance & Asset Health
key_responsibilities.description: Partner with maintenance and operations teams to improve asset care, failure analysis, spare planning, and uptime performance.

key_responsibilities.title: Process Improvement Delivery
key_responsibilities.description: Lead or sponsor engineering projects that improve throughput, reduce downtime, stabilize quality, or eliminate recurring technical losses.

key_responsibilities.title: Capex Planning & Execution
key_responsibilities.description: Shape the site engineering roadmap and support business cases for capex, automation, asset replacement, and process redesign.

key_responsibilities.title: Root Cause Analysis
key_responsibilities.description: Ensure that major breakdowns, recurring technical issues, and chronic line losses are investigated rigorously and translated into sustainable action.

key_responsibilities.title: Cross-functional Support
key_responsibilities.description: Work closely with Production, Quality, Supply Chain, IT, and Procurement on technical priorities that affect plant performance and change delivery.

key_responsibilities.title: Team Leadership
key_responsibilities.description: Lead and coach engineers and technical specialists; reinforce practical standards for ownership, documentation, and execution quality.

key_responsibilities.title: Illustrative KPIs
key_responsibilities.description: planned vs. unplanned downtime, MTBF/MTTR, engineering project delivery, line reliability, maintenance compliance, and technical loss reduction.

organizational_scope.Extent of Impact: Directly affects plant uptime, equipment stability, change execution quality, and technical readiness for growth and improvement.
organizational_scope.Decision Autonomy: Makes technical and engineering decisions within plant priorities, approved budgets, and safety/compliance standards; escalates major capex, structural, or risk-bearing technical changes.

reporting_relationships.Matrix/Functional Reporting: Dotted-line collaboration with Chief Operating Officer and IT on automation, technical systems, and plant modernization priorities.
reporting_relationships.Direct Reports (Number, Job Titles): Project and Process Engineers, planning/technical specialists, and selected engineering support roles
reporting_relationships.Dotted-line/Influence Relationships: Maintenance teams, Production leaders, Quality, Procurement, external contractors, and equipment suppliers.

technical_skills: Process Improvement & Technical Problem Solving - Advanced
technical_and_business_knowledge.business_knowledge_required: Strong understanding of how equipment stability, engineering standards, production planning, and quality expectations interact in a high-throughput manufacturing environment.

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/generated_final/Generated Job Descriptions Final/Head of Group Quality.docx

identity.Direct Reports: Quality managers, quality systems specialists, and selected quality experts across sites

job_descriptions.role_summary: Leads the Group quality agenda across food safety, compliance, quality systems, and operational quality performance. Sets the standards and governance needed to protect customers, brands, and operations while enabling scalable growth across sites and product categories.

key_responsibilities.title: Quality Strategy & Governance
key_responsibilities.description: Define and maintain the Group quality and food safety strategy, standards, and management routines across all relevant entities.

key_responsibilities.title: Quality Systems
key_responsibilities.description: Own the core quality management framework, including policy expectations, audit readiness, deviation handling, CAPA discipline, and documentation standards.

key_responsibilities.title: Compliance & Regulatory Coordination
key_responsibilities.description: Ensure that the organization remains aligned with relevant food safety, product, and regulatory requirements across markets and sites.

key_responsibilities.title: Operational Quality Performance
key_responsibilities.description: Partner with plants and functional leaders to improve quality performance, reduce recurring deviations, and strengthen preventive discipline.

key_responsibilities.title: Escalation & Incident Leadership
key_responsibilities.description: Lead or coordinate the response to significant quality incidents, audit findings, or compliance risks with material business impact.

key_responsibilities.title: Cross-functional Integration
key_responsibilities.description: Work closely with R&D, Operations, Procurement, Supply Chain, and Commercial where product, process, supplier, or customer requirements affect quality outcomes.

key_responsibilities.title: Capability Building

technical_skills: Continuous Improvement in Planning Processes - Advanced
technical_and_business_knowledge.business_knowledge_required: Strong understanding of line behavior, supply constraints, customer implications, and the practical costs of schedule instability in a production environment.

job_descriptions.relative_path: job_descriptions/generated_final/Generated Job Descriptions Final/Project and Process Engineer.docx

identity.Direct Reports: No direct reports; may coordinate technicians, operators, and external vendors within projects

job_descriptions.role_summary: Supports plant performance through structured process improvement, technical project delivery, and practical problem-solving on production systems. Combines engineering analysis with hands-on implementation to improve line stability, throughput, and execution quality.

key_responsibilities.title: Process Analysis
key_responsibilities.description: Analyze production processes, technical losses, bottlenecks, and workflow constraints to identify practical improvement opportunities.

key_responsibilities.title: Project Delivery
key_responsibilities.description: Lead or support engineering projects, modifications, and technical workstreams from scoping and documentation through implementation and handover.

key_responsibilities.title: Operational Troubleshooting
key_responsibilities.description: Partner with operations and maintenance teams on recurring technical issues, process instability, and line performance gaps.

key_responsibilities.title: Change Implementation
key_responsibilities.description: Prepare technical change documentation, coordinate relevant stakeholders, and ensure implementation is controlled, traceable, and practical for plant use.

key_responsibilities.title: Performance Improvement
key_responsibilities.description: Support initiatives to improve yield, changeover performance, throughput, process reliability, and operator usability.

key_responsibilities.title: Validation & Handover
key_responsibilities.description: Verify that process changes and technical interventions achieve intended results and are embedded into standards and documentation.

key_responsibilities.title: Cross-functional Coordination
key_responsibilities.description: Work with Quality, Production, Engineering, IT, and external vendors where process changes affect multiple workflows.

key_responsibilities.title: Illustrative KPIs
key_responsibilities.description: project milestones, process improvement impact, reduction in recurring losses, technical change cycle time, and implementation quality.

organizational_scope.Extent of Impact: Improves local manufacturing performance through targeted technical projects, process changes, and better execution discipline.
organizational_scope.Decision Autonomy: Makes recommendations and day-to-day engineering decisions within assigned projects and technical scope; escalates larger risk, budget, or plant-wide impact decisions to engineering leadership.

reporting_relationships.Matrix/Functional Reporting: Close project-based collaboration with Production, Maintenance, Quality, and Industrial IT.
reporting_relationships.Direct Reports (Number, Job Titles): No formal direct reports
reporting_relationships.Dotted-line/Influence Relationships: Operators, shift leaders, maintenance technicians, process specialists, and external technical suppliers involved in project execution.

technical_and_business_knowledge.business_knowledge_required: Good understanding of production flow, operator constraints, equipment dependencies, and the practical realities of implementing change in a live plant environment.
